shape_collection.description: Available primitive shapes and their characteristics

any.note: Should be avoided in favor of explicit union types

coerce_table_usage.description: Rules for using the coerce table from io.schema.json

safe.description: Coercion that never loses information or causes errors
examples: dict with extra keys -> dict with subset of keys

unsafe.description: Coercion that may lose information or cause runtime errors
examples: list -> list[string] (may fail if items not strings)
unsafe.behavior: Allowed but must be marked in edge.coerce field

forbidden.behavior: Reject graph validation, execution not allowed

required_ports.description: Ports that must be provided for node execution
required_ports.rule: If port.required is true, value must be non-null and match shape

optional_ports.rule: If port.required is false, null is acceptable, otherwise must match shape

default_values.rule: If port.default is defined, use when port not provided

explicit_variants.description: Union types must explicitly list all possible variants
explicit_variants.rule: Use union field with array of shape names, never use 'any'

variant_matching.description: How to match values against union variants
variant_matching.rule: Try variants in order, first match wins

nested_unions.rule: union[A, union[B, C]] becomes union[A, B, C]
